data.title: Record turnout with thousands of Santas surfing in Cocoa Beach on Christmas Eve 2025 presented by Orlando Health.
data.description: A popular holiday tradition unfolded on Florida's Space Coast in Cocoa Beach, as crowds gathered for the annual Surfing Santas event on Christmas Eve, December 24, 2025. Thousands of people flocked to the surfing capital to witness Santas riding epic waves in a picture-perfect setting. The festive event attracted a significant number of participants and onlookers, making it a vibrant celebration of the holiday season.

data.title: LG to reveal home robot LG CLOiD at CES 2026, aiming for "zero labor home." Redefines living with effortless chores.
data.description: At CES 2026 in Las Vegas, LG Electronics will introduce its latest home robot, the LG CLOiD, designed to simplify daily chores and provide more leisure time for users. This innovative product aims to redefine home living by offering a solution to mundane tasks.LG CLOiD is equipped with advanced technology to make household tasks effortless and give users the opportunity to prioritize their time more effectively. LG's introduction of the LG CLOiD at CES 2026 signifies a significant step in revolutionizing smart home living.

data.title: Titanium updates materials following OSC review, focuses on MD&A enhancements. (count: 78)
data.description: BOLTON, Ontario - On December 24, 2025, Titanium Transportation Group Inc. (TSX:TTNM, OTCQX:TTNMF) revealed changes made to its corporate presentation materials and upcoming Management’s Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) following a review by Ontario Securities Commission (OSC) officials. The OSC staff conducted a continuous disclosure review prompting the Company to make improvements, emphasizing transparency and quality in its reporting to stakeholders and investors. These modifications aim to provide detailed insights into the Company's performance and facilitate better decision-making based on accurate information.

data.title: Phison Electronics wins 2 awards in Taiwan FHIR Server Competition: Best Real-Time App and Best Hardware Performance.
data.description: In Taipei, Taiwan, on December 24, 2025, Phison Electronics (8299TT) disclosed its involvement in the first International FHIR Server Performance Competition organized by Taiwan’s Ministry of Health and Welfare (MOHW). Utilizing its in-house high-performance storage server solution, Phison was honored with two significant accolades - the Best Real-Time Application Award and the Best Hardware Performance.

data.title: Nvidia partners with Groq, an A.I. chip start-up. They will license technology and hire execs, expanding in the AI sector.
data.description: Nvidia has decided to license the technology developed by Groq and will also be bringing onboard its key executives. This move will further bolster Nvidia's position in the artificial intelligence chip market. The Silicon Valley giant's strategic decision highlights its commitment to advancing in this sector and capitalizing on the potential growth opportunities. This partnership signifies a significant development in the industry as Nvidia aims to harness Groq's expertise and strengthen its foothold in AI technology. The collaboration is expected to enhance Nvidia's product offerings and innovation capabilities, potentially reshaping the competitive landscape.

data.title: Gold and crude prices rise after U.S. actions towards Venezuela; gold hits record $4,440/oz, crude at $58/barrel. Oil tanker intercepted transporting Venezuelan crude, second seizure this month. Geopolitical tensions add risk premium to gold. U.S. Coast Guard pursuing another vessel under judicial seizure order. U.S. allies concerned.
data.description: On Monday, gold and crude oil both experienced a significant rise due to increasing U.S. actions towards Venezuela, causing geopolitical uncertainty in global markets. The iShares Gold Trust saw a 2.5% increase, reaching a new high near $4,440 per ounce. Similarly, West Texas Intermediate crude oil climbed 2.6%, hitting $58 per barrel - its strongest performance in two months. The spike in gold prices was fueled by recent events where U.S. forces seized an oil tanker carrying Venezuelan crude, the second such incident this month. The White House disclosed that the Coast Guard was pursuing another vessel allegedly operating under false pretenses and subject to a judicial seizure order. This situation has led to heightened geopolitical risks, prompting a gold price surge. Adam Turnquist, chief technical strategist at LPL Financial, commented on the potential for further escalation, especially involving Venezuela's allies like China and Russia. This news story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nvidia near key level; Apple, AppLovin, Palantir, Tesla in buy zones. Dow Jones Futures report.
data.description: Stock futures on the Dow Jones showed that Nvidia had surged close to an important resistance point on Monday. Additionally, Apple, AppLovin, Palantir, and Tesla were all identified as being in favorable buy zones. This information was reported without revealing the source, as the focus remains on the key details including the notable companies and their status in the market.

data.title: American Overseas Group Limited reported a net income of $2.8 million for the three months ending September 30, 2025. Book value per share increased to $1,209.44 compared to $969.27 in September 2024.
data.description: In Hamilton, Bermuda, on December 22, 2025, American Overseas Group Limited (BSX: AORE.BH) (Pink Sheets: AOREF.PK) (“AOG” or the “Company”) disclosed a consolidated net income available to common shareholders of $2.8 million, equivalent to $60.56 per diluted share, for the quarter concluding on September 30, 2025. This is in contrast to a consolidated net income available to common shareholders of $3.4 million, or $73.42 per diluted share, for the corresponding period in 2024. The book value per weighted share stood at $1,209.44 as of September 30, 2025, marking a rise from the $969.27 per weighted share noted at September 30, 2024.

data.title: Strategy video games like StarCraft II can delay neural aging by years, new research shows.
data.description: Recent studies have indicated that engaging in complex cognitive tasks such as playing StarCraft II or receiving musical training can potentially delay age-related declines in the brain by several years.
